attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- _sk-read-big-file: Read large files in manageable chunks when they exceed Claude's context limits. Use when working with large log files, data files, or when you get token limit errors reading files.
- _sk-cross-platform-sh: Review shell scripts for macOS + Linux compatibility. Use when the user asks to check, audit, or fix cross-platform issues in .sh files. Catches BSD vs GNU differences and bash version pitfalls.
- _sk-linear: Fetch Linear issue details and save to repo
- _sk-video-analyzer: Extract screenshots and transcribe audio from any video file
- _sk-penpot: Create and edit designs in Penpot via MCP. Use when user asks to create mockups, wireframes, UI designs, or interact with Penpot files.
- _sk-devpanel: Manage DevPanel VSCode extension config - add tasks, variables, replacements, git excludes. Use when user asks to configure DevPanel or add items to .devpanel/config.jsonc.
- _sk-sheet: Control Google Sheets - read/write cells, manage sheets, import/export data. Use when user asks to interact with spreadsheets.
- _sk-afk: Enable AFK mode - registers session for Telegram watch, enables remote communication via HTTP API. Use when user is going AFK and wants to keep interacting via Telegram.
- _sk-agent-cross-check: Ask another local AI agent session for an independent second opinion using omni-session, then synthesize the result.
- _sk-telegram-message: Send Telegram messages to the user via Bot API. Use when Claude Code needs to notify the user about task completion, errors, or any important event.
- _sk-chrome: Control Chrome browser - take screenshots, extract HTML, click elements, execute JS, get console logs. Use when user asks to interact with browser tabs.
- _sk-loom-download: Download Loom videos to local filesystem
- simple-skill: A minimal skill for testing. Just responds with a confirmation message.

- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Here's what I found about Vitest:

## Overview

Vitest (pronounced as "veetest") is a next generation testing framework powered by Vite. Vitest was created to make testing just work for Vite apps, and by building on top of Vite, Vitest natively understands your Vite config and is able to reuse the same resolve and transform pipelines.

## Key Features

Vitest offers smart & instant watch mode, native code coverage via v8 or istanbul, and Jest-compatible mocking, stubbing, and spies.

Vitest runs tests in parallel across worker threads for maximum speed, and it automatically enables an instant "watch" mode that reruns only the tests related to changed files.

Vitest has first-class support for modern JavaScript out of the box: it handles ESM, TypeScript, and JSX natively via Vite's transformer.

## Advantages

Vitest is Jest-compatible, which means you can reuse much of your existing test suite with minimal changes. Vitest can run tests more than four times faster than Jest in many scenarios.

Unlike Jest, which required Babel integration and TSJest setup, Vitest works out of the box and reuses your existing Vite configuration.

## Current Status

The latest version is 4.1.5, last published 12 days ago.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
